record.title: Kenneth Steffen , Interview #2 - MP3 Recording
record.subject: Latah County -- Idaho
record.origin: Father first came in the 1880's; parents moved from Kansas in 1900
record.keywords: stores schools work harvesting businesses childhood families farming doctors automobiles timber IWW jails veterans world wars railroads Chinese Americans-Americans politics childhood clothing orchards cemeteries liveries

point.title: Discarding tin container
point.synopsis: Getting money by hauling discarded tin containers to the hardware store in Moscow.

point.title: Early Moscow school
point.synopsis: Early Moscow school that he attended.

point.title: Rough work for farmers
point.synopsis: Rough work for farmers.

point.title: Raising three sons by himself; delivery business; home-owned groceries
point.synopsis: Raising three sons by himself after his wife died. His delivery business. Home-owned groceries in Moscow.

point.title: Working for the bakery; buying clothes
point.synopsis: Working for the bakery at twelve years of age. Buying clothes with earnings as a boy.

point.title: Wenatchee apple harvests; event from military history
point.synopsis: Working in Wenatchee apple harvests. An event from military history.

point.title: Masonic cemetery; Dr. Watkins didn't have professional training
point.synopsis: Mother gave flowers to people who were going to the cemetery. Will Steffen couldn't be buried in Masonic cemetery. Dr. Watkins, like other professionals of the time, didn't have professional training.

point.title: Old cars; rough roads; development of Inland Motor freight
point.synopsis: Old cars. Rough roads. Development of Inland Motor freight in the region.

point.title: Moscow wooden sidewalks; dislike of revivals; working in Oregon, cutting wood
point.synopsis: Moscow wooden sidewalks. Dislike of revivals. Working in Oregon, cutting wood, in 1924; then he re-enlisted.

point.title: Harvest work; working from town to town at various jobs; IWWs
point.synopsis: More harvest work: 33 horses on a combine. Free life of working from town to town at various jobs. Quitting on a farmer before he started. Smut and IWWs.

point.title: Minimum wage puts people out of work; son went to jail
point.synopsis: Minimum wage putting people out of work. He let son go to jail once.

point.title: Father's livery stable; wealthier people in Moscow; patriotic sacrifice
point.synopsis: Father's livery stable and others. He took farmers' excess hay for his ponies. Wealthier people in Moscow. Patriotic sacrifice carried too far.

point.title: Entertainment in Moscow; fatal accident at the VFW; delivering during World War II; Inland Freight
point.synopsis: Rooming on the road. Entertainment in Moscow - pool, cards, clubs. A fatal accident at the VFW. Delivering around the country during World War II. More about Inland Freight.

point.title: Knowing Eugene Settle
point.synopsis: Knowing Eugene Settle as a boy.

point.title: Riding the trains
point.synopsis: Riding the trains for free.

point.title: Guarding American freight on the Yangtze River
point.synopsis: Guarding American freight on the Yangtze River in China in the early thirties.

point.title: Chinese boy learned English on the boat; corruption in Chiang Kai Chek's government
point.synopsis: A Chinese boy learned English on the boat and got a bank job. Corruption in Chiang Kai Chek's government.

point.title: Spitting on the sidewalk; visiting a family in the country; walking to school; the family cow
point.synopsis: Farmers visited on sidewalks in Moscow - ordinance against spitting on sidewalk. Father always chewed tobacco. Visiting back and forth with a family in the country. Walking to school on crusted snow. Farmers got by by being self-sufficient. The family cow.

point.title: Father more considerate of his horses than kids; parents separated; mother worked in town
point.synopsis: Father was more considerate of his horses than his kids. Sometimes he was hungry as a boy. Mother worked in town after parents separated. She always went to church, but members never visited her when she was old.

point.title: Childhood had been tough; family history
point.synopsis: Father's teamster work after their separation. His own childhood had been tough. Family history - burials at the family plot in Pullman.

point.title: Will Steffen's death; father spoke German
point.synopsis: Will Steffen: his death and character. Father liked to speak German.

point.title: The Pastime, the local employment agency
point.synopsis: The Pastime was the local employment agency.

record.description: Varied work experiences. Moscow livery stables, freighting and pool halls. Parents. 1-13-76 1.3 hr
record.transcript: Download the PDF index; no transcript available
record.rights: Digital format copyright: University of Idaho Library. Original Copyright: Latah County Historical Society
